justification: All pass criteria met: 5 services recommended with thorough justification; Location Tracking identified as separate due to 5K writes/sec requiring TimescaleDB; Payment identified as separate due to strict consistency and PCI/compliance boundary; team size constraint explicitly factored (repeatedly warns against 6+ services for 8 engineers); Strangler Fig migration pattern recommended with detailed phased extraction order; trade_offs includes operational overhead (8 engineers maintaining 5 services) and distributed tracing complexity; risks includes distributed transaction/consistency problems (outbox pattern, reconciliation) and distributed monolith risk.

observations.text: Opens with the key insight: 5 services, not 6, explicitly calling out the team size constraint as the primary driver
Location Tracking correctly identified with specific infrastructure rationale (TimescaleDB for 5K writes/sec time-series data)
Payment correctly isolated with synchronous consistency requirement and ACL around external gateway
Customer accounts and notifications combined into one service with explicit acknowledgment that they might split later if notification volume grows
Strangler Fig migration with detailed 4-phase extraction order starting with Location (easiest boundary) then Payment
Distributed transaction risk addressed via outbox pattern, idempotency keys, and daily reconciliation jobs
Trade-offs table explicitly covers operational overhead, eventual consistency windows, and team skill gap
DDD context map with bounded contexts, aggregate roots, and relationship types (Customer-Supplier, Conformist, ACL)
Organizational risk called out: single point of knowledge failure with 1.5 engineers per service
Rigidity points section is a nice touch — explicitly marking intentional coupling (order state machine, payment sync)

agent_output_excerpt:
## Executive Summary

Decompose the monolith into **5 services** aligned to domain boundaries, not 6 (resist the urge to create one service per capability — that's a common mistake for an 8-person team). The critical insight: with 8 engineers and moderate operational maturity, over-decomposition will kill you faster than the monolith will. Start with 5 services, split further only when pain demands it. The location tracking service is the only component with fundamentally different infrastructur

grader_results.detail: missing values: trade_off, flexibility, bounded context
